items.text: Hi Marcus, Thanks for signing up for our memory research study. Your session is set for Thursday, April 9, at 10:00 AM in Whitfield Hall, Room 118. You'll receive $15 in cash when you finish. Please bring a pen. Best, Dr. Alvarez

questions.stem: According to the email, what is Marcus asked to bring to the session?

options.A: A writing instrument for the session
options.B: A printed copy of the schedule

questions.explanation: The email asks Marcus to bring a pen, which is a writing instrument.

questions.stem: In the email, the word "session" most nearly means a scheduled —
questions.explanation: The word "session" refers to the scheduled appointment for the research study set for Thursday at 10:00 AM.

items.text: Notice: During spring break, the Hartley Library will operate on reduced hours from March 16 to March 20. The building will be open from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM daily. The third-floor study rooms will remain closed. Regular hours resume Monday, March 23.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will be unavailable during spring break?
questions.explanation: The notice states that the third-floor study rooms will remain closed during spring break.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about library access on Monday, March 23?

options.A: The library will stay closed for cleaning
options.B: The library will follow its normal schedule
options.C: The library will open only to faculty
options.D: The library will extend its weekend hours

questions.explanation: Since regular hours resume on Monday, March 23, the library will follow its normal schedule that day.

items.text: USED TEXTBOOK SALE! Save big before classes start. Friday, August 28, in Carver Hall, Room 210. Doors open 11:00 AM. Hundreds of titles priced from $4 to $25. Cash only. Bring your course list and grab a deal early — popular books go fast!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the price range for the textbooks?
questions.explanation: The flyer states that titles are priced from $4 to $25.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how shoppers should pay at the sale?

options.A: They may charge purchases to a student account
options.B: They can pay using a mobile payment app
options.C: They should bring a personal check
options.D: They need to have physical currency ready

questions.explanation: Because the flyer specifies "Cash only," shoppers need to have physical currency ready to pay.

items.text: Notice: Fire alarm testing will be conducted in Brookside Residence Hall on Tuesday, May 5, from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM. Alarms will sound briefly during this window. No evacuation is required. A $25 fine will be issued to anyone who exits through emergency doors.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what penalty applies to residents who use the emergency doors?

options.B: A written warning from housing staff
options.C: A temporary loss of building access
options.D: A required meeting with the manager

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $25 fine will be issued to anyone who exits through emergency doors.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents during the testing window?

options.A: They must gather outside the building
options.B: They should silence the alarms themselves
options.C: They can remain inside their rooms
options.D: They have to report to the front desk

questions.explanation: Since the notice says no evacuation is required, residents can remain inside their rooms during the testing window.
